{{also|Brother|broГѕer}}
==English==
{{wp|<dab!>}}

===Alternative forms===
* {{alt|en|brotha|brudda||Jamaican English|AAVE}}
* {{alt|en|brothah|brothuh}}
* {{alt|en|bruvver||Cockney|MLE}}
* {{alt|en|broth'r||obsolete}}

===Etymology===
{{PIE word|en|bК°rГ©hв‚‚tД“r}}
{{etymon|en|:inh|enm:brother|tree=1}}
{{inh+|en|enm|broder}}, {{m|enm|brodir}}, {{m|enm|brother}}, {{m|enm|brothir}}, {{m|enm|broГѕer}}, {{m|enm|broГ°er}}, from {{inh|en|ang|brЕЌГѕor}}, {{m|ang|brЕЌГѕur}}, {{m|ang|brЕЌГ°er}}, {{m|ang|brЕЌГ°ur}}, from {{inh|en|gmw-pro|*brЕЌГѕer}}, from {{inh|en|gem-pro|*brЕЌГѕД“r|t=brother}}, from {{inh|en|ine-pro|*bК°rГ©hв‚‚tД“r|t=brother}}. {{dbt|en|bhai|bru|frater|friar|pal|vai}}.
{{col-top|2|cog}}
Cognate with {{cog|sco|breeder}}, {{m|sco|bridder}}, {{m|sco|brither}}, {{m|sco|broder}}, {{m|sco|brother}}, {{m|sco|bruther|t=brother}}, {{cog|yol|brover}}, {{m|yol|brower|t=brother}}, {{cog|frr|brouder}}, {{m|frr|bruler}}, {{m|frr|BrГ¶Д‘er|t=brother}}, {{cog|stq|Brour}}, {{m|stq|BrГєur|t=brother}}, {{cog|fy|broer|t=brother}}, {{cog|gsw|briГ¶der}}, {{m|gsw|bruder}}, {{m|gsw|brueder}}, {{m|gsw|brГ¶der}}, {{m|gsw|BrГјeder}}, {{m|gsw|BrГјГ¤dГ¤r|t=brother}}, {{cog|bar|pruadar}}, {{m|bar|prueder}}, {{m|bar|pruider|t=brother}}, {{cog|gmw-cfr|Broder|t=brother}}, {{cog|cim|pruadar}}, {{m|cim|pruudar|t=brother}}, {{cog|nl|broeder}}, {{m|nl|broer|t=brother}}, {{cog|de|Bruder|t=brother}}, {{cog|nds-de|Broor|t=brother}}, {{cog|li|broor}}, {{m|li|BroГ«r|t=brother}}, {{cog|lb|Brudder|t=brother; monk}}, {{cog|mhn|pruader|t=brother}}, {{cog|wym|brГјder|t=brother}}, {{cog|yi|Ч‘ЧЁЧ•Ч“ЧўЧЁ|t=brother}}, {{cog|da,nb,nn,sv|broder}}, {{m|sv|bror|t=brother}}, {{cog|ovd|bruoГ°er|t=brother}}, {{cog|fo,is|brГіГ°ir|t=brother}}, {{cog|gme-cgo|bruder|t=brother}}, {{cog|got|рђЊ±рђЌ‚рђЌ‰рђЊёрђЊ°рђЌ‚|t=brother}}; also {{cog|br|breur|t=brother}}, {{cog|kw|broder|t=brother}}, {{cog|ga|brГЎthair|t=brother}}, {{cog|gv|braar|t=brother; friar, monk}}, {{cog|gd|brГ thair|t=brother}}, {{cog|cy|brawd|t=brother}}, {{cog|la|frДЃter|t=brother; sibling}}, {{cog|grc|ОІПЃО¬|t=brother}}, {{m|grc|П†ПЃбѕ±МЃП„О·ПЃ|t=brother, citizen, clansman, kinsman}}, {{cog|xpg|ОІПЃО±П„ОµПЃОµ|t=brother}}, {{cog|xld|рђ¤Ўрђ¤­рђ¤ рђ¤±рђ¤­рђ¤ірђ¤¦рђ¤і|t=brother}}, {{cog|ltg|bruoДјs|t=brother}},  {{cog|lv|brДЃlis|t=brother}}, {{cog|lt|brГіlis|t=brother}}, {{cog|prg|brДЃti}}, {{m|prg|brote|t=brother}}, {{cog|be,bg,mk,ru,uk|Р±СЂР°С‚|t=brother}}, {{cog|cs|bratr|t=brother}}, {{cog|pl,sk,sl|brat|t=brother}}, {{cog|sh|Р±СЂР°МЏС‚}}, {{m|sh|brИЃt|t=brother; buddy, mate}}, {{cog|hy|ХЎХєХҐЦЂ}}, {{m|hy|ХЎХ­ХєХЎЦЂ}}, {{m|hy|ХЎХ­ХєХҐЦЂ}}, {{m|hy|ХҐХІХўХЎХµЦЂ|t=brother; buddy}}, {{cog|bal|ШЁШ±Ш§ШЄ|tr=barГЎt|t=brother}}, {{cog|ckb,sdh|ШЁШ±Ш§|t=brother}}, {{cog|kmr|bira|t=as a brother}}, {{cog|os|У•СЂРІР°Рґ}}, {{m|os|У•СЂРІР°РґУ•|t=brother}}, {{cog|ps|Щ€Ш±Щ€Ш±|tr=wror|t=brother}}, {{cog|fa|ШЁШ±Ш§ШЇШ±|tr=barДЃdar, birДЃdar / barГўdar}}, {{m|fa|ШЁШ±Ш§Ш±|tr=birДЃr / berГўr}}, {{m|fa|Щ€Ш±ШЇШ§Ш±|tr=vardГўr|t=brother; comrade; dude}}, {{cog|ask|bЕ™a|t=younger brother}}, {{cog|bsh|bЕ™o|t=brother}}, {{cog|trm|brДЃ|t=brother}}, {{cog|wbk|brДЃ}}, {{m|wbk|bЕ™ДЃ|t=brother}}, {{cog|xto|pracar|t=brother}}, {{cog|txb|procer|t=brother}}, {{cog|sa|а¤­аҐЌа¤°а¤ѕа¤¤аҐѓ|t=brother; friend}}.
{{col-bottom}}

===Pronunciation===
* {{IPA|en|/Л€bЙ№КЊГ°Й™/|a=RP}}
** {{audio|en|En-uk-brother.ogg|a=Southern England}}
* {{IPA|en|/Л€bЙ№КЊГ°Йљ/|a=GA,CA}}
** {{audio|en|LL-Q1860 (eng)-Naomi Persephone Amethyst (NaomiAmethyst)-brother.wav|a=North Carolina}}
** {{audio|en|en-us-brother.ogg|a=California}}
* {{IPA|en|/Л€bЙ№ЙђГ°Й™/|a=AU,NZ}}
* {{IPA|en|/Л€bЙ№КЉГ°Й™/||a=Northern England}}
** {{IPA|en|/bЙ№КЉЛ€Г°ЙњЛђ/||a=Bradford South Asian}}
* {{IPA|en|/Л€bЙ№КЉГ°Й™Й№/||a=Ireland}}
** {{IPA|en|/Л€bЙ№КЉdЙ™Й№/|/Л€bЙ№КЉdЙђ/|a=Dublin}}
* {{IPA|en|/Л€bЙ№КЊГ°Й™Й№/||a=Scotland,Northern Ireland}}
* {{IPA|en|/Л€bЙ№КЊvЙ™(Й№)/|a=th-fronting}}
* {{rhymes|en|КЊГ°Й™(Й№)|s=2}}

===Noun===
{{en-noun|s|brethren<l:<<archaic>> in most senses>}}

# {{senseid|en|Q10861465}}[[son|Son]] of the same parents as another person.
#* {{RQ:Maxwell Mirror and the Lamp|chapter=10|passage=It was a joy to snatch some brief respite, and find himself in the rectory drawingвЂ“room. Listening here was as pleasant as talking; just to watch was pleasant. The young priests who lived here wore cassocks and birettas; their faces were fine and mild, yet really strong, like the rector's face; and in their intercourse with him and his wife they seemed to be '''brothers'''.}}
# A male having at least one [[parent]] in common with another person (see [[half-brother]], [[stepbrother]]).
# A male fellow member of a [[religious]] community, [[church]], trades union etc.
#* '''1975''', {{w|New King James Version}}, Deuteronomy 23:19
#*: You shall not charge interest to your '''brother'''вЂ”interest on money ''or'' food ''or'' anything that is lent out at interest.
#: {{ux|en|Thank you, '''brother'''.}}
#: {{ux|en|I would like to thank the '''brother''' who just spoke.}}
# {{senseid|en|a man, addressed with respectful formula; form of address}} {{lb|en|informal}} {{n-g|A form of address to a man.}}
#: {{hypo|en|my brother in Christ}}
#: {{ux|en|'''Brother''', can you spare a dime?}}
#: {{ux|en|Listen, '''brother''', I don't know what you want, but IвЂ™m not interested.}}
# {{lb|en|AAVE}} A fellow [[black man#Noun|black man]].
#* {{quote-av|en|year=1987|speaker=Eddie Murphy|title=w:Eddie Murphy Raw|passage=[Michael Jackson] went on television and said, вЂњI don't have sex because of my religious beliefsвЂќ, and the public believed it. I know '''brothers''' was like вЂњget the fuck out of here!вЂќ. And white people, вЂњMichael's a special kinda guy!вЂќ}}
#* {{quote-journal|en|year=1991|month=January|journal=SPIN|volume=6|number=10|page=58
|passage=SPIN: Aren't you both as popular with white people as black people?<br>L.L.: Oh, no question. But I've always said, that's why when people say, "L.L., hey, like, on the last album, you sold out," I say, "Yo, can I ask you a question, Mike Tyson sell out?" "No, he's a '''brother'''." I say, he's a cross-over artist. He went pop. You know what I'm saying? I mean, the rap audience [...] they have to understand that their music is for all people. Me personally, I don't think it's about being black or white, {{...}}}}
#* {{RQ:Noire Thug-A-Licious|page=148|passage=The white cop grilled me. He was tall, but had a stomach like a pregnant woman. The other two were '''brothers''', and they looked like they just didn't wanna be standing there.}}
#* {{quote-book|en|year=2013|author=Gwyneth Bolton|title=Ready for Love|passage=But damn if they knew when to just leave a '''brother''' alone and let him sulk in silence.}}
# Somebody, usually male, connected by a common cause, situation, or affection.
#* {{quote-text|en|year=1963|author=Martin Luther King Jr.
|passage=The marvelous new militancy which has engulfed the Negro community must not lead us to a distrust of all white people, for many of our white '''brothers''', as evidenced by their presence here today, have come to realize that their destiny is tied up with our destiny.}}
#* {{quote-book|en|year=2016| title=The City of My Brothers|author=William Burkholder | page=| passage=O, then! To ride upon such glories, Till my time comes nigh, And commune in the city of peaceful slumbers Among my '''brothers''' of wind-blown rye.}}
# Someone who is a [[peer]], whether male or female.
#* {{RQ:Orwell Animal Farm|1
|passage=And, above all, no animal must ever tyrannise over his own kind. Weak or strong, clever or simple, we are all '''brothers'''.}}
# {{lb|en|poetic}} Someone who is a [[kinsman]] or shares the same [[patriarch]].
#* {{quote-book|en|year=1995| title=Conjuring Culture|author=Theophus H. Smith| page=89| passage=The eighteenth century text, with its antislavery message and its Adamic figuration, calls implicily for the reconciliation of all peoples as "'''brothers'''" (not the reprehensible brothers of Joseph but the cocreated '''brothers''' of Adam).}}
#* {{quote-journal|en|year=1908|month=June|work=National Magazine |title=A Keeper of the Door|author=Grace Kellogg|volume=28|page=280|passage=Oh, my '''Brothers''', five nights ago many of our braves were out upon the buffalo grounds.}}
#* {{quote-book|en|year=2010| title=Introduction to Tribal Legal Studies |author=Justin B. Richland; Sarah Deer | page=193| passage=In the case of the boy, a certain amount of instruction comes from the male members of the mother's clan, such as how to go after game, how to handle horses, how to dress, how to conduct yourself and what to seek in life. They also teach the boy how to treat domestic animals.  Even pets understand kindness, and the clan '''brothers''' use that as an example.}}
#* {{quote-book|en|year=2020| title=Three Kingdoms: Super Hegemon|author=Xiao Xiao Ma Jia Hao | page=| passage=The carriage that the '''brothers''' of the Kai clan rode on had travelled a lot these past few days, and the horses that pulled the carriage were exhausted.}}

====Usage notes====
* The plural вЂњbrethrenвЂќ (cf. вЂњ[[sistren]]вЂќ, вЂњ[[sistern]]вЂќ) is not used for biological brothers in contemporary English (although it was in older usage). It still finds use, however, in the meaning of вЂњmembers of a religious orderвЂќ. It is also sometimes used in other figurative senses, e.g. вЂњadherents of the same religionвЂќ, вЂњcountrymenвЂќ, and the like.

====Hypernyms====
* {{sense|son of common parents}} {{l|en|sibling}}

====Coordinate terms====
* {{sense|with regards to gender}} {{l|en|sister}}

====Derived terms====
{{q|Abbreviations}}: [[bro]], [[brah]], [[bra]], [[bruh]], [[bruv]]
{{col4|en|title=single words|brotherboy|brotherdom|brotherfucker|brotherfucking|brotherji|brotherlocks|brotherman|brotherness|brotherred|brothership|bruncle|godbrother|merbrother|outbrother|swordbrother|unbrother|unbrothered
|brothered|my brother in Christ
|brotherhood
|brotherless
|brotherlike
|brotherly
|co-brother
}}
{{col4|en|title=Compound words and expressions|collapse=n
|[[big brother]] / {{nowrap|[[Big Brother]]}}
|blood brother|brother in Christ|sugar brother
|brother german
|brother-in-arms|Bob's your mother's brother
|brother-in-law
|Brother Jonathan|baby brother|Bob's your father's brother|Robert is your father's brother|Robert is your mother's brother|Robert's your mother's brother
|Christian Brother
|[[cousin brother]] / {{nowrap|[[cousin-brother]]}}
|everyone and their brother,everybody and their brother
|[[foster brother]] / {{nowrap|[[foster-brother]]}}
|[[half brother]] / {{nowrap|[[half-brother]]}}
|lay brother
|little brother
|Middle Brother
|milk brother
|North Brother
|soul brother
|[[stepbrother]] / {{nowrap|[[step-brother]]}}
|The Brothers
|Xaverian Brother
|3/4 brother|brother chip|brother from another mother|brother in law|brother-german|brother-husband|brother-officer|brother-out-law|brother-slayer|brother-zone|co-brother-in-law|everybody and his brother|everyone and his brother|full-brother|good-brother|half brother-in-law|kid brother|milk-brother|oath-brother|older brother|Robert's your father's brother|three-quarter brother|womb-brother|younger brother}}

====Related terms====
{{col4|en
|friar
|fraternal
|fraternity
}}

====Descendants====
{{top3}}
* {{desc|bah|bredda}}
* {{desc|bzj|breda}}
* {{desc|bi|brata}}
* {{desc|wes|broМ±da}}
* {{desc|gul|broda}}
* {{desctree|hwc|braddah}}
* {{desc|icr|broda}}
* {{desc|kri|brohda}}
* {{desc|bzk|brada}}
* {{desc|pcm|broda}}
* {{desc|fpe|brЙ”da}}
* {{desc|pis|brata}}
* {{desc|srm|baГЎa}}
* {{desctree|srn|brada}}
* {{desc|tpi|brata|barata}}
* {{desc|it|bro|bor=1}}
* {{desc|ja|гѓ–гѓ©г‚¶гѓј|bor=1}}
* {{desc|kea|brГіda|bor=1}}
* {{desc|ko|лёЊлќјлЌ”|bor=1}}
* {{desc|pt|brada|brother|brГіder|bor=1}}
{{bottom}}

====Translations====
{{see translation subpage|Noun}}

===Verb===
{{en-verb}}

# {{lb|en|transitive}} To treat as a brother.
#* {{quote-text|en|year=1819|author=w:Walter Scott|title=w:Ivanhoe
|passage=Seest thou not we are overreached, and that our proposed mode of communicating with our friends without has been disconcerted by this same motley gentleman thou art so fond to '''brother'''?}}

====Translations====
{{see translation subpage|Verb}}

===Interjection===
{{en-intj}}

# {{non-gloss|Expressing exasperation.}}
#: {{ux|en|We're being forced to work overtime? Oh, '''brother'''!}}

{{cln|en|endearing terms|terms of address}}
{{C|en|Male family members|Monasticism}}
{{cln|en|nouns with irregular plurals}}

==Middle English==

===Alternative forms===
* {{alter|enm|broder|brodir|brodyr|brothir|brothre|brothur|broГѕer|broГѕir|broГѕre|broГѕur}}
* {{alter|enm|broiГѕer|bruther||Northern}}
* {{alter|enm|broГ°er||early}}; {{alter|enm|broГѕerr||Orm}}

===Etymology===
{{etymon|enm|:inh|ang:brЕЌГѕor}}
{{inh+|enm|ang|brЕЌГѕor}}, from {{inh|enm|gmw-pro|*brЕЌГѕer}}, from {{inh|enm|gem-pro|*brЕЌГѕД“r}}, from {{inh|enm|ine-pro|*bК°rГ©hв‚‚tД“r}}. {{doublet|enm|frere}}. Forms with {{IPAchar|/d/}} both represent a phonological development and analogy with {{m|enm|fader|t=father}}, {{m|enm|moder|t=mother}}.

{{col-top|2|Development of the plural}}
Plural forms with {{IPAchar|/eЛђ/}} are from late {{m+|ang|brД“Гѕre}}, with generalization of the mutated vowel from the Old English dative singular {{m|ang|brД“Гѕer}} by analogy with other nouns with mutation in both the dative singular and nominative/accusative plural such as {{m|ang|fЕЌt}}, {{m|ang|tЕЌГѕ}} ({{m|enm|fot}} and {{m|enm|toth}}), perhaps reinforced by the {{w|Old Norse}} plural {{m|non|brЗїГ°r}}. By the end of the Early Middle English period, the final ''-e'' of this form was either lost ({{m|enm|brether}}), in conformance with both the consonantal pattern of the singular and a tendency for {{IPAchar|/(Й™)lЙ™/|/(Й™)nЙ™/|/(Й™)rЙ™/}} to develop into {{IPAchar|/Й™l/|/Й™n/|/Й™r/}}, or replaced with {{m|enm|-en|id=noun plural}} ({{m|enm|bretheren}}) as part of the spread of (nominative/accusative) plurals in ''-en'' at the expense of less salient ''-e''. The same processes explain the supplanting of early {{m+|enm|broГѕre}}, {{m|enm|broГ°re}} ({{m+|ang|brЕЌГѕra}}, {{m|ang|brЕЌГѕru}}) with ''brother'' and {{m|enm|brotheren}}, though ''brother'' may also continue  the Old English null plural {{m|ang|brЕЌГѕor}}. Finally, the forms {{m|enm|brotheres}} and {{m|enm|bretheres}} plainly represent adaptation to the regular plural in {{m|enm|-es|id=noun plural}}.
{{col-bottom}}

===Pronunciation===
* {{IPA|enm|/Л€broЛђГ°Й™r/|/Л€broЛђdЙ™r/}}

===Noun===
{{enm-noun|gen=brother|gen2=brotheres|pl=bretheren|pl2=brether|pl3=brotheren}}

# A {{l|en|brother}} or [[brother-in-law]]; a [[male]] sibling.
#* {{RQ:Ormulum|d|3|url=https://digital.bodleian.ox.ac.uk/objects/90a06f70-880a-4b5b-bd30-798710afff11/surfaces/a2980d0a-ac52-4383-ba1b-bc0a6a6f567a/|Nu broГѕerr З·allкћ‡Н›. '''broГѕerН¬''' min. Affкћ‡Н› Гѕe flГ¦sheЕїЕї kД©deв№Ћ вЃЉ broГѕerН¬ mД±n Д± crД±ЕїЕїtenndom. Гћurrh fulluhhtв№Ћ<!--?--> вЃЉ Гѕurrh troкџ•Гѕeв№Ћ вЃЉ broГѕerr mД±n Д± кџ‘odeЕїЕї huЕї {{...}}|Now, Brother Walter, my '''brother''' by blood relation and in Christendom, through baptism and faith, and my brother in God's house {{...}}}}
# A [[blood brother]]; a [[companion]] in a two-member [[loyalty]] [[pact]].
# Any [[male]] [[relative]] or [[family]] [[member]].
# A [[colleague]] or [[peer]] {{gloss|sometimes as a [[term of address]]}}:
## A [[fellow]] {{gloss|[[male]]}} [[human]] [[being]].
## A [[male]] [[friend]] or [[companion]].
## A [[male]] [[fellow]] [[Christian]] {{gloss|or other [[coreligionist]]}}.
##* {{RQ:Wycliffe NT Lichfield|Revelation|1|9|117|v|column=2|I ioon Иќoure '''bкќ›oГѕer''' вЃЉ partener in tribulacioun вЃЉ kingdom вЃЉ pacience in criЕїt iheЕїu .В· was in an ile Гѕat is clepid pathmos В· foкќ› Гѕe woкќ›d of god В· вЃЉ foкќ› Гѕe witneЕїЕїyng of iheЕїu|I, John, your '''brother''' and partner in tribulation, the Kingdom, and endurance in Jesus Christ, was on an island that's called Patmos for the word of God and for the witnessing of Jesus.}}
## A [[male]] [[fellow]] [[member]] of a [[guild]] or [[religious]] [[order]].
## {{lb|enm|rare}} A [[male]] [[fellow]] [[ruler]].
# [[something|Something]] [[similar]] or [[resemblant]].

====Usage notes====
* The plural form {{m|enm|brotheres}} also occurs, though less commonly. Furthermore, one occasionally encounters {{m|enm|brother}}/{{m|enm|brothere}} (especially in Early Middle English) and {{m|enm|bretheres}} (especially in Late Middle English).

====Declension====
{{enm-decl-noun|brother|[[brother]], [[brotheres]]||[[brotheren]], [[brether]]([[bretheren|en]])|[[brotheren]], [[brether]]([[bretheren|en]])}}

====Related terms====
* {{l|enm|brotherhede}}
* {{l|enm|brother-in-lawe}}
* {{l|enm|brotherles}}
* {{l|enm|brotherly}}
* {{l|enm|brotherwort}}

====Descendants====
* {{desc|en|brother}} {{see desc}}
*: Ottawa Valley: {{desc|en|bruder|nolang=1}}
* {{desctree|gmw-msc|brother|noalts=1}}
* {{desc|yol|brover|alts=1}}

====References====
* {{R:MED Online|entry=brб»ЌМ„ther|pos=n|id=MED6165|accessdate=2019-02-21}}

{{topics|enm|Crafts|Male family members|Monarchy|Religion}}
{{cln|enm|consonant stem nouns|nouns with invariant genitive singulars|weak nouns}}

==Middle Scots==

===Alternative forms===
* {{alter|gmw-msc|broder|brodir|brodyr|brodyre|brothyre|bruder|brudir|bruther|bruthir}}

===Etymology===
{{inh+|gmw-msc|enm-esc|brothir}}, from {{inh|gmw-msc|ang|brЕЌГѕor}}, from {{inh|gmw-msc|gmw-pro|*brЕЌГѕer}}, from {{inh|gmw-msc|gem-pro|*brЕЌГѕД“r}}, from {{inh|gmw-msc|ine-pro|*bК°rГ©hв‚‚tД“r}}.

===Pronunciation===
* {{IPA|gmw-msc|/Л€brГёЛђГ°Й™r/|/Л€brГёЛђdЙ™r/}}

===Noun===
{{head|gmw-msc|noun|plural|brether|or|brethren}}

# A {{l|en|brother}}; a [[male]] [[sibling]].
# A [[colleague]] or [[companion]].
# A [[fellow]] [[member]] of an [[organisation]].

====Descendants====
* {{desc|sco|brither|bruther|broder|bruder}}

===References===
* {{R:Dictionary of the Scots Language|brother, bruther|source=DOST|url=https://dsl.ac.uk/entry/dost/dost00054280}}
* {{R:Dictionary of the Scots Language|broder, bruder|source=DOST|url=https://dsl.ac.uk/entry/dost/broder_n}}

==Old Frisian==

===Etymology===
{{dercat|ofs|gem-pro|inh=1}}
From {{inh|ofs|gmw-pro|*brЕЌГѕer}}.

===Noun===
{{ofs-noun|head=brЕЌther|m}}

# {{l|en|brother}}

====Descendants====
* {{desc|frr|bruder|qq1=Amrum|bruler|qq2=FГ¶hr|broor|brГ¶Г¶r|qq3=Goesharde (Hoolmer)|brГҐr|qq4=Goesharde (Hoorninger)|brГ¶Г¶Г°er|qq5=Goesharder (Southern)|qq6=Halligen|Bruur|qq7=Heligoland|brouder|qq8=Mooring|BrГ¶Д‘er|qq9=Sylt}}
* {{desc|stq|BrГєur|alts=1}}
* {{desc|fy|broer}}

==Portuguese==

===Etymology===
{{ety|pt|:bor|en:brother|text=+|tree=1}} {{doublet|pt|frade|freire|frei|bro|brada}}.

===Pronunciation===
{{pt-IPA|br=brГіder}}

===Noun===
{{pt-noun|m|#s}}

# {{lb|pt|slang}} [[bro]] {{gloss|close friend}}
#: {{uxi|pt|Esse cara Г© o meu '''brother'''.|That guy is my '''bro'''.}}

====Derived terms====
* {{l|pt|brotheragem}}

==Scots==

===Noun===
{{head|sco|noun}}

# {{alt form|sco|brither}}

===References===
* {{R:Dictionary of the Scots Language|entry=brother|pos=n|url=https://dsl.ac.uk/entry/dost/dost00054280|accessdate=23 May 2024|source=DOST}}

==Spanish==

===Etymology===
{{ety|es|:bor|en:brother|text=+|tree=1}}

===Pronunciation===
{{es-pr|brГіder|hyph=+}}

===Noun===
{{es-noun|m|#s}}

# {{lb|es|colloquial|US|Puerto Rico|Honduras|El Salvador|Nicaragua|Bolivia|Spain}} [[bro]], [[dude]], {{l|en|brother}}
#: {{syn|es|cabrГіn<q:vulgar>|mano#Etymology_2_6}}

===Further reading===
* {{R:es:DA}}
